title: Caffeine and anxiety documentation

investigations.text: Supports caffeine, nicotine, medication, substance, sleep-apnoea, restless-legs, pain, withdrawal, thyroid, dyspnoea, mood, anxiety, life-stress, bedtime-worry, environmental, and sleep-habit review plus selected tests when clinically indicated.
investigations.rationale: Supports clinician-entered substance and sleep-context documentation for caffeine and anxiety documentation; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports caffeine, nicotine, medication, substance, sleep-apnoea, restless-legs, pain, withdrawal, thyroid, dyspnoea, mood, anxiety, life-stress, bedtime-worry, environmental, and sleep-habit review plus selected tests when clinically indicated.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Insomnia

treatment_and_management.text: Supports anxiety and worry history, duration, distress, functional impairment, comorbid depression or anxiety, substance use, medical and treatment history, education, and active monitoring.
treatment_and_management.rationale: Supports anxiety symptoms, duration, function, comorbidity and monitoring for caffeine and anxiety documentation; no decision is generated.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Caffeine product, amount, timing, pattern, anxiety and sleep symptoms, temporal relation, function, mental state, risk, discussion and plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for caffeine and anxiety documentation.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
